nodes.name: Webhook
nodes.notes: Production URL: https://[your-instance].app.n8n.cloud/webhook/vapi-escalation — set this as the server URL in the create_escalation tool definition in VAPI.

parameters.subject: =[Escalation] New support request — {{ $('Extract Escalation Data').first().json.callerName }}
parameters.message:
=

New Escalation — RelayPay Voice Agent

A customer has been escalated from the voice support agent and requires follow-up.

Caller Name{{ $('Extract Escalation Data').first().json.callerName }}
Email{{ $('Extract Escalation Data').first().json.callerEmail }}
Category{{ $('Extract Escalation Data').first().json.category }}
Preferred Callback Time{{ $('Extract Escalation Data').first().json.preferredTime }}
Reason{{ $('Extract Escalation Data').first().json.escalationReason }}
Call ID{{ $('Extract Escalation Data').first().json.callId }}
Timestamp (WAT){{ $('Extract Escalation Data').first().json.timestampReadable }}

Please follow up with the caller at their preferred time. This record has been logged in Airtable.

parameters.subject: [URGENT] Escalation workflow failed — manual follow-up required

meta.description: Synchronous VAPI tool call handler for escalation. Receives escalation data from VAPI (caller name, email, preferred time, category, reason), creates an Airtable record in the Escalations table, sends a notification email to the support team, and returns a confirmation message back to VAPI. Includes an Error Trigger for fallback notification if the workflow fails.
meta.notes: SETUP: (1) Replace all credential IDs. (2) Set env vars: AIRTABLE_BASE_ID, SMTP_FROM_EMAIL, ESCALATION_EMAIL. (3) Configure the Error Trigger by setting this workflow as its own error workflow in Settings.
